name: W8A8
oneliner: Both weights and activations quantized to 8-bit — saves memory AND compute via integer arithmetic. Standard for data-center serving.
explanation: W8A8 quantization stores both weights and activations in 8-bit integer format, enabling integer matrix multiplication on tensor cores. Unlike weight-only quantization which only saves memory bandwidth, W8A8 provides both memory savings and a compute speedup because both operands of the matrix multiply are quantized. The challenge is that activations must be quantized at runtime, which is harder due to input-dependent distributions and outlier values. SmoothQuant makes this practical by redistributing quantization difficulty from activations to weights.
fundamentals: Inference: 1) Quantize activation: X_int8 = round(X_fp16/s_x). 2) Read int8 weights. 3) INT8 GEMM: Y_int32 = X_int8 @ W_int8. 4) Dequantize: Y_fp16 = Y_int32 $\times$ s_x $\times$ s_w. Key difference from W4A16: saves both bandwidth AND compute. For large batch sizes (compute-bound workloads), W8A8 provides real speedup while W4A16 does not.
